criteria.condition: Protected state can be mutated outside its transition gate.
Scope or principal can be taken from untrusted input.
Achieved is reachable without independent evidence.
An external action can execute without complete authorization.
Budget can be charged twice or become negative.
Locks can be released while an external effect may remain unresolved.
A non-idempotent ambiguous action can be retried automatically.
Audit and the protected domain transition are not atomic.
Startup or restore can report ready=true before reconciliation.
A kill switch can be bypassed.
Backup or restore has not been proven.

rules: Unknown evaluation state is treated as a triggered release stop.
Policy, approval, a later delivery stage, or an operational workaround cannot waive a criterion.
A triggered criterion blocks the affected implementation and every dependent release gate until objective evidence proves it false.
